FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Prediction markets hit record volumes on World Cup betting; Citadel's hedge funds post solid H1 gains; mostly corporate calendar items.

bullets.text: Prediction market platforms Kalshi, Polymarket, and Rothera all hit record trading volumes in June, with Rothera alone moving $2B on World Cup betting.
Citadel's hedge funds gained broadly in H1 2026, with tactical trading strategy up 14.3% YTD, outperforming amid broader quant volatility.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Vestmark hits $50B AUM inflection point on retail UMA demand; litigation and tech certifications round out the week.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP platform crossed $50B AUM (5x growth) after launching retail UMA with tax management; T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, VanEck now referring clients—clear signal standalone RIA tech is in demand.
Edelman Financial Engines appealing federal court rejection of its trade-secrets claim against Mariner Holdings; judge found no evidence of misappropriation.
Orion earned ISO 42001 AI certification for its advisor platform, joining Nitrogen in a small global cohort—standards validation for wealth tech infrastructure.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Sidecar capital floods the market as reinsurance pricing faces structural headwinds; tech adoption accelerates across underwriting.

bullets.text: Sidecar reserves jumped 64% to $90bn+ in 2025, driven by strong US annuity sales—a structural shift adding capacity to an already oversupplied market.
JP Morgan sees no pricing floor through 2026–27 despite Q2 gains; lighter hurricane seasons and oversupply mean sustained margin compression ahead.
Reinsurers are customizing coverage and expanding flexible structures at US property renewals, signaling they're competing aggressively for business.
40% of insurers now deploy AI in underwriting as competitive pressure forces automation—a critical efficiency lever in a margin-squeezed environment.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Four stories on crypto infrastructure expansion and emerging risks—from stablecoin scaling to regulatory warnings on tokenization and AI agents.

bullets.text: Standard Chartered partners with Circle to offer institutional clients direct USDC minting/redemption, signaling bank appetite for embedded stablecoin infrastructure.
Hayfin closed €15bn fifth direct lending fund with more conservative structuring after US semi-liquid vehicle challenges; shifting LP appetites toward stability.
IMF warns tokenization will migrate financial risk to less-regulated service providers, requiring updates to monetary-policy frameworks—early institutional pushback on crypto migration.
MAS and Singapore banks release AI agent safeguards white paper, establishing first-mover regulatory framework as autonomous systems become active in trading and settlement.

PE & Deals.lede: Four stories tracking PE capital deployment: BCI launches LP-direct capital solutions, Evercore bulks up Asia secondaries talent, Sandbrook bets €200M on European data centers, and Wellesley Retirement Board seeks investment consultant.

bullets.text: BCI launching capital solutions strategy to let LPs directly participate in continuation vehicles and secondaries—positioning itself ahead of trend, though most LPs still outsource.
Evercore hired secondaries specialist in Asia, replacing departing Singapore director; signals bulge-bracket intensifying competition for LP capital in secondary fund-of-funds.
Sandbrook deployed €200M into Krios (European data-center platform) on back of AI compute demand; PE appetite for digital infra continues to accelerate.

Markets & Macro.lede: 4 stories flagging market structure risks: PE deal friction, tech repricing, narrowing breadth masking rally, and earnings forecasts outpacing fundamentals.

bullets.text: KKR/Bridgepoint's £5.7bn DCC Energy bid faces shareholder revolt from Ninety One, Aviva, and Fidelity—deal execution risk rising.
Tech valuations deteriorating sharply; FT warns old-fashioned valuation discipline will outperform as repricing accelerates.
S&P 500 earnings forecasts rising at fastest pace since 2020—but equity gains mask narrowing breadth and AI-concentration, signaling regime change risk beneath surface.
Oil contango persisting through 2026 structurally depressing passive commodity ETF returns as monthly roll-downs lock in losses.

Newsletters.lede: Marc Rubinstein on valuation stretching and market dynamics.

bullets.text: Rubinstein examines how valuations are expanding in certain pockets of the market, signaling potential overextension in growth narratives.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories spanning geopolitics, demography, and biotech: American dominance fueled global prosperity, but Britain's infrastructure rot mirrors Mississippi's decline while birth rate drops may actually boost per-capita growth—and GLP-1 drugs could rewire human behavior.

bullets.text: Britain's economic stagnation now matches Mississippi per-capita income; the culprit is chronic underinvestment in infrastructure and services, not accounting illusions.
Falling birth rates correlate with *higher* per-capita GDP growth—contradicting conventional wisdom that demographic decline cripples economies.
Developed economies face demographic urgency and need skilled migration to sustain growth and tax bases; polarized immigration rhetoric is obscuring the economic case.
GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic may reshape psychology and behavior at scale—affecting appetite, ambition, risk-taking, and social dynamics beyond weight loss.

all.lede: 5 stories: TAMP platform hits milestone, stablecoin infrastructure expands, and alternative credit raises record capital.

bullets.text: Vestmark's TAMP platform crosses $50B AUM (5x growth) after launching retail UMA with tax management; T. Rowe Price, BlackRock, Capital Group, VanEck now referring clients—signals strong RIA tech demand.
Standard Chartered partners with Circle to offer institutional clients direct USDC minting/redemption, expanding stablecoin infrastructure for digital asset trading.
Hayfin closes €15bn fifth direct lending fund, moving to more conservative structuring after US semi-liquid vehicle headwinds.
Visa launches cyber threat intelligence platform and executes agentic transactions with European banks, pushing deeper into software/services beyond payments.
